op.name: mintypecode
op.notes: Return minimum data type character that can satisfy all given types.
op.summary: Return the character for the minimum-size type to which given types can be safely cast.

blocks.text:
Return the character for the minimum-size type to which given types can
be safely cast.

The returned type character must represent the smallest size dtype such
that an array of the returned type can handle the data from an array of
all types in `typechars` (or if `typechars` is an array, then its
dtype.char).

desc_blocks.text:
If a list of strings, each string should represent a dtype.
If array_like, the character representation of the array dtype is used.

items.name: typeset
items.type: str or list of str, optional

desc_blocks.text:
The set of characters that the returned character is chosen from.
The default set is 'GDFgdf'.

The default character, this is returned if none of the characters in
`typechars` matches a character in `typeset`.
The character representing the minimum-size type that was found.

blocks.code:
>>> import numpy as np
>>> np.mintypecode(['d', 'f', 'S'])
'd'
>>> x = np.array([1.1, 2-3.j])
>>> np.mintypecode(x)
'D'
